query:
INSERT INTO quiz_questions (lessonId, question, questionType, options, correctAnswer, questionOrder) VALUES
-- DIV111 Lesson 1: Project Planning and Research Methods (360031)
(360031, 'What is the primary purpose of a capstone project in theological education?', 'multiple_choice', '["To fill time","To integrate and apply learning","To memorize facts","To compete with peers"]', 'To integrate and apply learning', 1),
(360031, 'Which research method involves collecting data through interviews?', 'multiple_choice', '["Quantitative only","Qualitative","Neither","Statistical analysis"]', 'Qualitative', 2),
(360031, 'A thesis statement should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Vague and general","Clear, specific, and arguable","As long as possible","Copied from sources"]', 'Clear, specific, and arguable', 3),
(360031, 'Primary sources in theological research include:', 'multiple_choice', '["Commentaries","Encyclopedia articles","Original biblical texts and manuscripts","Wikipedia"]', 'Original biblical texts and manuscripts', 4),
(360031, 'What is the purpose of a literature review?', 'multiple_choice', '["To fill pages","To survey existing scholarship on your topic","To avoid research","To copy others work"]', 'To survey existing scholarship on your topic', 5),
(360031, 'APA and Turabian are examples of:', 'multiple_choice', '["Bible translations","Citation styles","Preaching methods","Prayer techniques"]', 'Citation styles', 6),
(360031, 'A research question should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Impossible to answer","Focused and researchable","Already answered","Irrelevant to theology"]', 'Focused and researchable', 7),
(360031, 'Plagiarism is:', 'multiple_choice', '["Acceptable in ministry","Using others work without attribution","Required in research","A minor issue"]', 'Using others work without attribution', 8),
(360031, 'The methodology section of a project explains:', 'multiple_choice', '["Your conclusions","How you conducted your research","Your personal opinions","Nothing important"]', 'How you conducted your research', 9),
(360031, 'Peer review in academic work involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Self-grading","Evaluation by qualified scholars","Ignoring feedback","Automatic approval"]', 'Evaluation by qualified scholars', 10),
(360031, 'Describe the key components of a well-designed capstone project and explain why each is important.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'A capstone project includes clear objectives, sound methodology, thorough research, and practical application.', 11),

-- DIV111 Lesson 2: Biblical Exegesis Methods (360032)
(360032, 'Exegesis means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Reading into the text","Drawing meaning out of the text","Ignoring the text","Rewriting the text"]', 'Drawing meaning out of the text', 1),
(360032, 'The grammatical-historical method focuses on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Modern interpretations only","Original language and historical context","Personal feelings","Popular opinions"]', 'Original language and historical context', 2),
(360032, 'What is the difference between exegesis and eisegesis?', 'multiple_choice', '["No difference","Exegesis draws out meaning, eisegesis reads meaning in","They are synonyms","Both are wrong"]', 'Exegesis draws out meaning, eisegesis reads meaning in', 3),
(360032, 'Context in biblical interpretation includes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only the verse itself","Literary, historical, and cultural context","Modern context only","No context needed"]', 'Literary, historical, and cultural context', 4),
(360032, 'Word studies in exegesis involve:', 'multiple_choice', '["English dictionaries only","Analyzing original Hebrew and Greek terms","Guessing meanings","Ignoring definitions"]', 'Analyzing original Hebrew and Greek terms', 5),
(360032, 'The Sitz im Leben refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["A German theologian","The life setting or context of a passage","A Bible translation","A prayer method"]', 'The life setting or context of a passage', 6),
(360032, 'Genre analysis helps interpreters understand:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing useful","How different types of literature should be read","Only poetry","Modern novels"]', 'How different types of literature should be read', 7),
(360032, 'The principle of Scripture interpreting Scripture means:', 'multiple_choice', '["Ignore difficult passages","Use clearer passages to illuminate unclear ones","Each verse stands alone","Contradiction is acceptable"]', 'Use clearer passages to illuminate unclear ones', 8),
(360032, 'Textual criticism deals with:', 'multiple_choice', '["Criticizing the Bible","Establishing the original text from manuscripts","Modern translations only","Personal opinions"]', 'Establishing the original text from manuscripts', 9),
(360032, 'Application in exegesis bridges:', 'multiple_choice', '["Nothing","The ancient text and contemporary life","Only academic concerns","Past and future"]', 'The ancient text and contemporary life', 10),
(360032, 'Explain the steps of sound biblical exegesis and why proper methodology matters for theological study.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Sound exegesis involves observation, interpretation, and application using proper hermeneutical principles.', 11),

-- DIV111 Lesson 3: Theological Integration (360033)
(360033, 'Theological integration involves:', 'multiple_choice', '["Keeping disciplines separate","Connecting biblical, theological, and practical knowledge","Ignoring theology","Only academic study"]', 'Connecting biblical, theological, and practical knowledge', 1),
(360033, 'Systematic theology organizes doctrine by:', 'multiple_choice', '["Random order","Topical categories","Chronological order only","Personal preference"]', 'Topical categories', 2),
(360033, 'Biblical theology emphasizes:', 'multiple_choice', '["Modern categories","The progressive revelation of Scripture","Ignoring the Old Testament","Only the Epistles"]', 'The progressive revelation of Scripture', 3),
(360033, 'Practical theology focuses on:', 'multiple_choice', '["Theory only","Ministry application and practice","Abstract concepts","Academic debates only"]', 'Ministry application and practice', 4),
(360033, 'A theological framework helps:', 'multiple_choice', '["Confuse issues","Organize and evaluate beliefs consistently","Avoid thinking","Ignore Scripture"]', 'Organize and evaluate beliefs consistently', 5),
(360033, 'Orthodoxy refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Wrong belief","Right or correct belief","No belief","Personal opinion"]', 'Right or correct belief', 6),
(360033, 'Orthopraxy refers to:', 'multiple_choice', '["Right belief","Right practice or action","Wrong practice","Theoretical knowledge"]', 'Right practice or action', 7),
(360033, 'The relationship between doctrine and life should be:', 'multiple_choice', '["Disconnected","Integrated and transformative","Theoretical only","Irrelevant"]', 'Integrated and transformative', 8),
(360033, 'Worldview analysis examines:', 'multiple_choice', '["Only Christian views","Underlying assumptions and beliefs that shape thinking","Nothing important","Scientific data only"]', 'Underlying assumptions and beliefs that shape thinking', 9),
(360033, 'Apologetics in theological integration defends:', 'multiple_choice', '["Personal opinions","The reasonableness of Christian faith","Secular philosophy","Cultural trends"]', 'The reasonableness of Christian faith', 10),
(360033, 'Discuss how theological integration enhances ministry effectiveness and personal spiritual formation.', 'short_answer', '[]', 'Theological integration connects knowledge with practice, leading to transformed lives and effective ministry.', 11);
